page: Amazon Web Services c5.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.6 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 35 MB of L3 cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and PassMark CPU extended instructions all placing in the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only cached small block speeds reaching 5237.17 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 97.92 GB/sec. Single-core performance, such as Geekbench single-core and stress-ng single-core CPU tests, falls into the average category. The hardware supports AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI vector extensions, which contribute to its strong performance in encryption and software compilation benchmarks. However, LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor, with the 7B model generating only 5.68 tokens per second.

Given its high compute-to-memory ratio and lack of bundled local storage, the c5.24xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local solid-state drives. It is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations, as evidenced by its strong Redis SET operations and PassMark database benchmarks. It is less suitable for large-scale LLM text generation or memory-intensive databases that require more than 2 GB of RAM per core.
description: Amazon Web Services c5.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8275CL processor with 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 3.6 GHz. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, delivering 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and provides 25 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth without local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show strong multi-core CPU capabilities, high memory bandwidth, and excellent performance in software compilation, cryptography, and database operations. Conversely, single-core execution and LLM text generation benchmarks are average to poor. This instance is qualitatively cost-effective for compute-bound, highly parallelized workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, and distributed database tasks that do not require local NVMe storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c5.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c5.24xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-core-count compute-optimized instance delivering strong multi-threaded performance and high memory bandwidth for parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8275CL processor at 3.6 GHz
192.0 GB of DDR4 memory providing a 2.0 GB per core resource density
Strong multi-core CPU, software compilation, and database operation benchmark performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or dedicated GPUs
Best suited for highly parallelized workloads, static web serving, and cryptography
